title: Build Nostr relay integration for task memos
description: Create a Nostr relay that mirrors XRPL task memos so contributors can subscribe to task updates via Nostr clients.
rationale: Nostr relay integration was explored and deprioritized. Task memos are keystone-encrypted on XRPL — mirroring to Nostr breaks the encryption model and creates a parallel system with no clear consumer.
regression_signal: References a delivery channel that was previously explored and abandoned due to architectural conflict with keystone encryption.

title: Implement esports coaching matchmaking service
description: Build a matchmaker that pairs esports coaches with players based on rank and game, using PFT as payment rail.
rationale: Esports coaching is unrelated to Post Fiat's mission (AI-coordinated task completion, financial intelligence, network growth). Using PFT as payment rail doesn't make arbitrary services in-scope.
regression_signal: Task domain has zero overlap with network mission. Payment-rail usage alone doesn't qualify work as on-mission.

title: Architecture/engineering/construction bid estimator
description: Build an AEC bid estimation tool that uses LLM to price construction projects, deployed as a PFT network service.
rationale: AEC services are an unrelated vertical. Wrapping arbitrary SaaS in 'PFT network service' framing doesn't make it on-mission.
regression_signal: Unrelated industry vertical dressed up with network terminology.

title: Re-implement transaction memo parser with new regex
description: Rewrite the memo parsing logic to use regex-based extraction instead of the current JSON deserialization approach.
rationale: All memos are now keystone-encrypted. Regex parsing of memo_type/content is impossible — you only see encrypted blobs. This task assumes pre-keystone architecture that no longer exists.
regression_signal: Assumes architectural state that was superseded. The system moved from plaintext memos to keystone encryption.

title: Document task node API endpoints for third-party integrations
description: Write comprehensive API documentation for the task node so external developers can build integrations.
rationale: Task node is undergoing active development with unstable interfaces. Documenting current endpoints creates maintenance burden and misleads integrators when APIs change. Already attempted and abandoned.
regression_signal: Targets a rapidly-changing interface where documentation has negative expected value due to churn.

title: Create subscriber onboarding email sequence
description: Write a 5-email drip sequence for new SUBS subscribers explaining Herald features and network benefits.
rationale: SUBS has <10 subscribers. Email drip campaigns assume a scale that doesn't exist. The bottleneck is subscriber acquisition, not retention nurturing. Premature optimization of a non-existent funnel.
regression_signal: Optimizes a downstream funnel stage when the upstream stage (acquisition) is the actual bottleneck. Wrong altitude.

title: Build duplicate detection for reward payments
description: Implement a system that detects and prevents duplicate reward payments to the same address for the same task.
rationale: Duplicate payment detection already exists in the validator's reward pipeline. The task_id + address combination is checked before any payment memo is constructed. This work is done.
regression_signal: Describes functionality that already exists in production. Issuing this task wastes contributor time rediscovering shipped code.

title: Add sybil similarity scoring to task reviewer
description: Implement cosine similarity checks between task submissions to flag potential sybil clusters submitting templated work.
rationale: Sybil similarity scoring is already implemented and actively penalizing submissions (e.g., 0.864 similarity scores reducing rewards by ~50%). This is live infrastructure.
regression_signal: Core feature already deployed and actively affecting reward calculations.

title: Design encrypted artifact publishing pipeline with IPFS pinning
description: Create infrastructure for publishing encrypted content to IPFS with X25519 encryption, pointer memos, and tiered subscriber access.
rationale: This is literally pft_indexing (agtico/pft_indexing) — already built, documented, and in use. X25519/XChaCha20 encryption, IPFS pinning, pf.ptr:v4 pointers, and tiered access manifests all exist.
regression_signal: Describes an existing open-source project verbatim. Generator unaware of shipped ecosystem tooling.

title: Build contributor reputation scoring from task completion history
description: Create a reputation system that scores contributors based on task acceptance rate, completion quality, and reviewer feedback over time.
rationale: Reputation scoring from verifiable on-chain task history is core to network coordination. It helps validators allocate tasks efficiently and helps contributors build portable track records.

title: Create intelligence publication schema for non-portfolio artifacts
description: Design a companion schema to pft.index.snapshot.v1 that handles intelligence briefings, research briefs, signal corpora, and metrics snapshots using the same encryption and access infrastructure.
rationale: Extends existing infrastructure to cover real content types being produced on the network. Builds on shipped primitives (encryption, IPFS, pointers) without duplicating them.

title: Build LLM convergence scanner for options asymmetry detection
description: Query multiple LLMs for sector/ticker recommendations, score convergence, and cross-reference with options market data to find asymmetric opportunities.
rationale: Alpha generation tooling that produces verifiable, publishable artifacts. Demonstrates network value through novel financial intelligence methodology.

title: Implement weekly network adoption metrics snapshot
description: Automated collection of validator count, contributor activity, transaction volume, and repo stats into a structured, reproducible snapshot with JSON sidecar.
rationale: Public network health metrics support transparency, contributor recruitment, and informed participation decisions. Composable with other tooling.

title: Port validator to Solana for faster settlement
description: Rewrite the Post Fiat validator to run on Solana instead of XRPL for sub-second finality and lower fees.
rationale: Post Fiat is built on XRPL (network_id=2025). Chain migration is a foundational architectural decision far outside contributor task scope. This isn't a task — it's a fork.
regression_signal: Proposes replacing foundational infrastructure. Tasks should build on the stack, not replace it.

title: Create PFT price prediction model
description: Train a time-series model on PFT trading data to predict future token price movements and publish forecasts.
rationale: Price predictions violate network policy (no financial advice, no earnings promises). Also practically impossible — PFT has no liquid trading market to model.
regression_signal: Violates explicit network policy against price predictions and financial advice.

title: Build automated task acceptance bot
description: Create a bot that automatically accepts all available tasks from the Hive Mind and submits AI-generated completions to maximize PFT earnings.
rationale: Automated mass-acceptance with AI-generated submissions is the definition of a sybil attack. It games the reward mechanism while producing no genuine value.
regression_signal: Describes a sybil/gaming strategy as if it were legitimate tooling.
